mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods when FEDFUNDS < 2.0% do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth does not correlate positively with 60-day forward SPX returns (Spearman ρ < 0.10), or the relationship reverses in post-2020 data.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering a negative equity risk premium adjustment that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: Instances of simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST declines do not produce negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% annualized signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, supporting corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day forward window.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO growth > 0.125% do not exceed median returns by at least 50 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.5% year-over-year, combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 3.0%, signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression that creates equity valuation pressure through expected future rate hikes, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods meeting both trigger conditions do not show negative bias (mean return < 0) at p < 0.10, or the interaction coefficient is not significant in a regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market weakening or early recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods when UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline), or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% do not exceed median returns by at least 75 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1.2 million units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining signals a demand-side shock that reduces forward equity valuations through lower expected consumer spending and construction activity, producing negative 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.2M annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month change ≤ 0%
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods meeting both trigger conditions do not show negative bias (mean return < 0) at p < 0.10, or the joint effect is not statistically stronger than either signal alone.
